title: Java Class Basics
description: Type a minimal Java class with a main method.

choices: Nothing, because main is not the entry point
Compilation fails because main returns void
completionChecks.explanation: main is the entry point and prints "Ready"; a void return is exactly what a Java main method must declare.

completionChecks.prompt: Which method signature is the standard Java application entry point shown here?
completionChecks.explanation: The JVM launches a public static void main that takes a String[] parameter; the others have the wrong modifiers, return type, or parameter.

completionChecks.prompt: What happens if Demo changes main to public void main(String[] args) and you run java Demo?
choices: It prints Ready because public is enough for the launcher
It compiles, but the launcher does not treat the instance method as the entry point
Compilation fails because void methods cannot be named main
The JVM creates a Demo instance and calls main automatically
completionChecks.explanation: The method can compile as an instance method, but the Java launcher needs the static main signature shown in the lesson.
